hints.text: Plates white or standard EU blue-strip; **taxis = yellow plates**
**Greek alphabet** — unique; lowercase has softer shapes than Cyrillic (Cyprus also uses it)
Poles primarily **wooden, dark brown & tall**; **harp-shaped metal poletops** (unique to GR + Cyprus); 5 vertical insulators
Faded **solid white double middle lines**; **yellow road lines** more common than in any other S. European country
Bollards wedge-shaped w/ almost-square reflectors (red front / white back)
Chevrons **white arrows on black**; warning signs red border + yellow background
Directional signs blue, arrow-shaped: **Greek in yellow, English in white**
Town-entry signs white w/ thick blue horizontal stripes; km markers blue w/ "km" in Latin + Greek
Pedestrian sign = **two dotted horizontal lines** (not vertical stripes)
**Miniature roadside churches** on small pedestals — unique to Greece
White/pastel stone houses w/ orange roofs; iconic **white houses + blue trim** (mainly on the islands)
Very **mountainous** (winding mountain roads); **red dirt** common (also Albania); bleak Gen3 winter/fall coverage

Area codes start **2** (+1–3 digits; 3rd digit 1 = biggest city, 9 = its surroundings); **Cyclades** = white+blue houses, barren treeless small islands; **Meteora** monoliths N of Trikala; Athens yellow / Thessaloniki blue taxis
